concerns.text: FR-005 and Plan Summary explicitly require the acoustic baseline to be constructed by 're-running the original EVA-Bench pipeline on the same dataset with acoustic perturbations.' Task T028 only describes 'loading acoustic baseline data,' which implies using pre-existing external data rather than performing the required re-execution. This leaves the core requirement of generating the baseline via the pipeline unaddressed.

SC-002 requires the system to report 'No threshold detected' with a 'confidence interval' if the piecewise regression fails. Task T023 handles the fallback to linear regression but does not explicitly mandate the calculation and reporting of the confidence interval for this specific failure case, leaving a gap in the success criterion coverage.

FR-001 and SC-001 require injecting delays in '200ms increments' across the full range (200ms–2000ms). Task T015 only implements a batch loop for a '1000ms delay' to verify tractability. There is no task explicitly covering the implementation and verification of the full 200ms–2000ms sweep in the injection phase, leaving the core requirement of the latency sweep partially unaddressed.

SC-003 and FR-008 require the final report to explicitly state whether the latency threshold is 'statistically distinct' based on an 'interaction test or t-test.' Task T032 only mandates generating a conclusion based on 'ANOVA results' generally, without explicitly requiring the specific interaction test or t-test defined in the requirements to validate that distinctness.

SC-004 and FR-007 require the full dataset analysis to complete within 6 hours on a CPU-only runner. While T009 sets up 'CPU-only execution constraints,' there is no specific task to verify or benchmark that the 6-hour constraint is met (e.g., a performance validation task) before proceeding to the final phases, risking a failure in SC-004 that isn't caught until integration.
